dimensions.name: Detective Layer Implementation
dimensions.description: Evaluates the implementation of forensic tools for evidence collection from the repository using robust parsing (AST), sandboxed cloning, and strict state management.
dimensions.forensic_instruction: Scan src/tools/ for cloning logic and src/state.py (or equivalent) for state definitions. Use AST parsing (not regex) to verify: (1) tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() or equivalent sandboxing for git clone; (2) subprocess.run() with error handling, no os.system(); (3) state uses Annotated with operator.add/operator.ior as reducers for parallel safety; (4) Evidence and JudicialOpinion as Pydantic BaseModel. Capture code snippets.
dimensions.success_pattern: Robust evidence collection using proper AST parsing, sandboxed git clone with error handling, and strict state management with reducers. All protocols from the rubric fully met.
dimensions.failure_pattern: Missing forensic tool implementation, raw os.system() calls, no sandboxing, plain dicts for state without reducers, or minimal file existence checks only.

dimensions.name: Graph Orchestration Architecture
dimensions.description: Assesses the design and implementation of the StateGraph architecture including parallel fan-out/fan-in, conditional edges, and error handling of the orchestration flow.
dimensions.forensic_instruction: Scan src/graph.py for StateGraph builder. Use AST to analyze add_edge() and add_conditional_edges(). Verify: Detectives (RepoInvestigator, DocAnalyst, VisionInspector) fan-out from one node and run concurrently; synchronization node (EvidenceAggregator or equivalent) collects evidence before Judges; Judges (Prosecutor, Defense, TechLead) fan-out in parallel and fan-in before ChiefJustice; conditional edges for Evidence Missing / Node Failure.
dimensions.success_pattern: StateGraph fully implements parallel execution for detectives and judges, synchronization nodes, and conditional edge handling as per requirements.
dimensions.failure_pattern: Purely linear flow, no parallel branches, no synchronization node, no conditional edges for error handling. No StateGraph architecture.

dimensions.name: Judicial Persona Differentiation & Structured Output
dimensions.description: Evaluates the implementation of distinct judicial agents (Prosecutor, Defense, Tech Lead) with clear separation in system prompts and structured JSON output enforcement.
dimensions.forensic_instruction: Scan src/nodes/judges.py. Verify: (1) Prosecutor, Defense, Tech Lead have distinct adversarial system prompts (Prosecutor: gaps/security/laziness; Defense: effort/intent/workarounds; Tech Lead: architecture/maintainability). (2) LLMs invoked with .with_structured_output() or equivalent bound to JudicialOpinion schema (score, argument, cited_evidence). (3) Retry/error handling for malformed output.
dimensions.success_pattern: All three judge personas distinctly implemented with adversarial prompts and enforced structured output (.with_structured_output or equivalent) meeting the rubric.
dimensions.failure_pattern: No distinct judge agents, shared prompts, no structured output enforcement, freeform text parsing without Pydantic validation.

dimensions.name: Chief Justice Synthesis Engine
dimensions.description: Measures the implementation of a deterministic synthesis engine that aggregates judge opinions with hardcoded conflict resolution rules and produces a detailed final verdict.
dimensions.forensic_instruction: Scan src/nodes/justice.py for ChiefJusticeNode. Verify hardcoded deterministic Python logic (not LLM-only): (1) Security override - confirmed vulnerability caps score at 3; (2) Evidence supremacy - Defense hallucination overruled when evidence contradicts; (3) Functionality weight - Tech Lead architecture confirmation for Graph criterion; (4) Score variance > 2 triggers re-evaluation. Output is Markdown report.
dimensions.success_pattern: Implements hardcoded if/else conflict resolution, security override, fact supremacy, dissent requirement, and detailed Markdown report output.
dimensions.failure_pattern: ChiefJustice is just an LLM averaging scores. No hardcoded rules. No dissent summary. Console output only.

dimensions.name: Generated Audit Report Artifacts
dimensions.description: Assesses the final audit report output generated from the agent workflow. The report should be a production-grade Markdown file with executive summary, criterion breakdown, and remediation plan.
dimensions.forensic_instruction: Verify the audit report generation in src/nodes/justice.py produces a Markdown file with: Executive Summary, Criterion Breakdown (including dissent summaries per dimension), and actionable Remediation Plan. Check output path and serialization logic.
dimensions.success_pattern: Fully structured Markdown report with detailed executive summary, criterion breakdown (including dissent summaries), and actionable remediation steps.
dimensions.failure_pattern: No generated audit report, minimal/unstructured output, missing required sections, or no actionable remediation instructions.

synthesis_rules.security_override: Confirmed security flaws (os.system, shell injection, path traversal, unsanitized input, no sandbox/tempfile) cap the total score at 3 regardless of Defense arguments.
synthesis_rules.fact_supremacy: Forensic evidence from Detectives always overrules Judicial opinion. If Defense claims features but evidence shows missing, overrule for hallucination.
synthesis_rules.functionality_weight: If Tech Lead confirms architecture is modular and workable, this carries highest weight for Graph Orchestration criterion.
synthesis_rules.dissent_requirement: Chief Justice must summarize Prosecutor vs Defense disagreement. Every criterion with score variance > 2 must include explicit dissent explanation.
synthesis_rules.variance_re_evaluation: If score variance across judges exceeds 2 for any criterion, trigger re-evaluation of cited evidence before rendering final score.
